name: when-analyzing-skill-gaps-use-skill-gap-analyzer
description: Analyze skill library to identify coverage gaps, redundant overlaps, optimization opportunities, and provide recommendations for skill portfolio improvement
capabilities: Map domain coverage and identify missing capabilities
Track skill usage metrics and patterns
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Pure documentation and analysis skill with no executable code. Only reads local SKILL.md files from ~/.claude/skills/ directory. No network calls, no external commands, no environment access beyond documented skill paths. Static findings are false positives from misinterpreting GraphViz diagram syntax and markdown code block delimiters as security threats.
valueStatement: Skill libraries often grow organically, creating gaps and redundancies. This skill systematically analyzes your skill portfolio to identify missing capabilities, duplicate functionality, and optimization opportunities. Get actionable recommendations to improve your skill ecosystem.
limitations: Analyzes only local SKILL.md files in ~/.claude/skills/
Does not create or modify skills automatically
Requires claude-flow hooks and memory MCP for full integration
Does not access external APIs or remote skill repositories

useCases.title: Portfolio Health Check
useCases.description: Conduct quarterly reviews of skill coverage, identify gaps, and plan new skill development

useCases.title: Before Adding New Skills
useCases.description: Analyze existing capabilities to avoid redundancy and ensure new skills fill real gaps

useCases.title: Architecture Optimization
useCases.description: Identify over-complex skills that should be broken down and find composability improvements

promptTemplates.title: Basic Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the skill gap analyzer to scan my skill library and identify the main coverage gaps and redundancies

promptTemplates.title: Scenario Coverage
promptTemplates.prompt: Run scenario coverage analysis to see if my skill library can handle full-stack web app development and ML pipeline deployment

promptTemplates.title: Optimization Focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my skill library for under-utilized skills and over-complex skills that need refactoring

promptTemplates.title: Full Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a complete skill gap analysis: inventory, coverage gaps, redundancy detection, optimization opportunities, and generate a prioritized recommendation report

bestPractices: Run quarterly analysis to maintain healthy skill coverage above 85%
Address critical gaps first to achieve 90% domain coverage goals
Consolidate skills with over 70% overlap to reduce redundancy below 10%
antiPatterns: Adding new skills without checking for existing overlapping capabilities
Ignoring under-utilized skills (below 5% usage) for too long
Allowing complex skills with low success rates to remain unimproved

faq.question: Which AI tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex. Requires claude-flow for full hook integration.

faq.question: How many skills can it analyze?
faq.answer: Handles any size library. Large libraries (50+ skills) use sampling and clustering for efficiency.

faq.question: Does it integrate with other tools?
faq.answer: Yes, works with claude-flow hooks, memory MCP, and spawns researcher agent for optimization analysis.

faq.question: Is my skill data safe?
faq.answer: Yes, only reads local SKILL.md files. No data is sent externally or stored outside configured memory.

faq.question: Why are some skills marked over-complex?
faq.answer: Skills averaging over 5000 tokens with below 70% success rate are flagged for simplification or decomposition.

faq.question: How is this different from prompt optimization?
faq.answer: Skill gap analyzer evaluates portfolio-level health. Prompt optimization analyzes individual skill effectiveness.
